findings.title: The objective effect is scale-dependent
findings.summary: Pure-MLM vs hybrid MLM+CLM: neutral at 10M (3-seed 63.58±1.73 vs 64.09±0.26, overlapping CIs) but +5.49pp at 100M (73.06 vs 67.57). The CLM head's dilution of MLM is a scale-dependent bottleneck.

findings.title: Kāraka masking is causally null at matched budget
findings.summary: Arm K (kāraka-stratified masking) vs Arm C (uniform random) at identical mask budget, 100M, N-hot/RoPE/objective/corpus fixed. Targeted 20-paradigm subset: 82.03 vs 81.93 (ΔK−C +0.10, 95% CI [−0.99, +1.20], ns). Full BLiMP 71.77 vs 70.49. The masking distribution has no causal effect; the apparent gain was a freq-weighting / mask-rate confound.

findings.title: Kāraka auxiliary objective: weak, plausibly-specific lever
findings.summary: Śābdabodha aux head (predict kāraka roles, λ=1.0) vs matched no-aux baseline, 3 seeds, uniform masking. Targeted 74.41±0.82 vs 72.95±0.68 (Δ+1.46); overall BLiMP 65.37 vs 63.74 (+1.63). Consistent direction across seeds but not significant at 3 seeds. A shuffled-role specificity control (3 seeds) does NOT reproduce the effect (shuf−base +0.58/−0.05), arguing against generic regularization and weakly favouring a kāraka-specific mechanism. Needs more seeds / 100M to resolve.

strict_track.objective: pure-MLM (RoPE, Vidyut N-hot, kāraka masking, Muon)
strict_track.note: Robust wins attributed to objective (pure-MLM) + architecture (RoPE); Pāṇinian mechanisms are small, non-harmful, interpretable additions (see F2/F3), not the primary driver.

submission_track.note: Hybrid model retained for the 10M submission (pure-MLM pivot only warranted at 100M, per F1).
